coverage_requirements: Documented failure of conservative treatment (minimum 4-6 weeks)
Physical therapy trial documented (minimum 6 sessions)
Red flag symptoms present (radiculopathy, progressive neurological deficit, cauda equina syndrome) OR failure to improve with conservative management
Clinical correlation with physical examination findings
auto_approve_criteria: Conservative treatment failure >= 6 weeks AND radiculopathy on exam
denial_criteria: Duplicate imaging within 6 months without clinical change
references: CMS LCD L35028 - MRI of the Spine
ACR Appropriateness Criteria - Low Back Pain (2024)
AHRQ Guideline - Noninvasive Treatments for Low Back Pain

coverage_criteria.procedure_description: Rhinoplasty, primary; lateral and alar cartilages and/or elevation of nasal tip
coverage_requirements: Documented functional impairment (nasal airway obstruction)
Failed trial of medical management (nasal steroids, antihistamines)
Objective testing (rhinomanometry or acoustic rhinometry) showing obstruction
Pre-authorization photos showing deformity IF reconstructive
auto_approve_criteria: Documented nasal obstruction with objective testing AND failed medical therapy
Post-traumatic nasal deformity with functional impairment
denial_criteria: Diagnosis-procedure mismatch (e.g., skin condition diagnosis for nasal surgery)

coverage_criteria.procedure_description: Lumbar arthrodesis (spinal fusion), posterior or posterolateral technique
coverage_requirements: Documented failure of conservative treatment (minimum 3-6 months)
Physical therapy (minimum 12 sessions or 6 weeks)
At least one epidural steroid injection trial
Imaging correlation (MRI/CT) showing structural pathology
Psychological clearance for chronic pain patients
BMI < 40 (relative contraindication above)
Hemoglobin A1C < 8.0% for diabetic patients
auto_approve_criteria: Spondylolisthesis Grade II+ with neurological deficit
Failed conservative care >= 6 months with all documentation complete
references: CMS LCD L37950 - Lumbar Spinal Fusion

coverage_requirements: Confirmed diagnosis of indicated condition (RA, PsA, AS, Crohn's, UC)
Documentation of disease severity (DAS28 score for RA)
Failed adequate trial of conventional DMARDs (methotrexate at 15-25mg/week for >= 3 months)
Laboratory results: RF, anti-CCP, ESR, CRP within 3 months
TB screening (PPD or QuantiFERON) within 12 months
Hepatitis B/C screening
Biosimilar step therapy consideration
auto_approve_criteria: DAS28 >= 5.1 AND failed methotrexate 20mg/week >= 3 months with documentation
references: CMS LCD L33822 - Biologic DMARDs for Autoimmune Conditions

coverage_requirements: Biopsy-confirmed malignancy with FDA-approved indication
Biomarker testing (PD-L1 TPS for NSCLC, MSI/dMMR for applicable cancers)
ECOG performance status 0-2
Absence of contraindicated mutations (EGFR, ALK for NSCLC first-line mono)
Staging documentation
Prior treatment history for non-first-line indications
auto_approve_criteria: NSCLC with PD-L1 TPS >= 50% AND no EGFR/ALK AND ECOG 0-1 (NCCN Category 1)
MSI-H/dMMR solid tumor after prior treatment
Urgent/emergent oncology with NCCN Category 1 evidence
denial_criteria: Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression
Non-FDA-approved indication without clinical trial enrollment
references: NCCN Guidelines - Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer v4.2026
CMS-0057-F - Prior Authorization and Interoperability Final Rule (72-hour SLA)
